title: Workflow Manifest
description: Schema for shared/workflow-manifest.json — single source of truth for specification-derived workflow constructs. Structural constraints are validated here; semantic cross-reference checks (unique role IDs, fail_routing references valid role IDs, default_stages is a subset of canonical_order) are enforced by scripts/validate-workflow-manifest.js.

spec_version.description: Workflow specification version this manifest encodes (semver)

roles.description: All workflow agent roles. Each role must have a unique id, name, and number (enforced by validate-workflow-manifest.js).

id.description: Machine-friendly role identifier. Stable once assigned. Used as graph stage name and config key.

name.description: Display name for the role (matches AGENT_ROLES in constants.ts)

number.description: 1-based ordinal role number. Reflects position in the workflow sequence.

orchestrating.description: True if this role orchestrates the workflow (e.g. Planner, Synthesis). Orchestrating roles have pipeline: null.

pipeline.description: Pipeline type this role owns (e.g. 'implementation'), or null for orchestrating roles or roles that do not own a pipeline (e.g. Project Manager). Must reference a value in pipelines.canonical_order when non-null (enforced by validate-workflow-manifest.js).

persona_file.description: Relative path from the workspace root to the role's Claude Code persona file

persona_file_deep_agents.description: Relative path from the workspace root to the role's deep-agents persona file

pipelines.description: Pipeline type definitions: ordering, default subset, prerequisites, and fail routing.

canonical_order.description: All pipeline types in canonical execution order. Must be a superset of default_stages (enforced by validate-workflow-manifest.js).

default_stages.description: Default pipeline subset (the 4-stage legacy default per spec §3.2). Must be a subset of canonical_order (enforced by validate-workflow-manifest.js).

prerequisites.description: For each pipeline type in canonical_order, the pipeline that must PASS before it can start. null means no prerequisite. Keys must cover all canonical_order types (enforced by validate-workflow-manifest.js).

fail_routing.description: For each pipeline type, the role ID of the agent who fixes FAIL outcomes. Values must reference valid role IDs (enforced by validate-workflow-manifest.js).

statuses.description: Status vocabularies for each entity type. Consumers apply their own type treatment (Zod enums in TS, frozensets in Python).

terminal_work_package.description: Subset of work_package statuses that are terminal — no further agent action is required. Must be a non-empty subset of work_package (enforced by validate-workflow-manifest.js).

constants.description: Specification-defined workflow tuning constants (§7, §8, §18). Changing a constant requires editing this file only.

max_rework_count.description: Maximum number of rework cycles allowed per pipeline type per work package

stale_pipeline_hours.description: Hours after which an IN_PROGRESS pipeline is considered stale

max_handoff_depth.description: Default maximum auto-handoff depth before the workflow terminates

handoff_depth_multiplier.description: Multiplier applied to work package count to compute effective max handoff depth
